ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT EVERS' MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT ELLIOT EVERS' MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. MOTION IS LARGELY MOOT BECAUSE THE COURT HAS OVERRULED DEFENDANT EVERS' DEMURRER (SEE AUGUST 29, 2013 ORDER OVERRULING DEFENDANT EVERS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, NOTICE OF ENTRY INTO THE DOCKET ON SEPTEMBER 4, 2013). PLAINTIFF SCHATZ IS ENTITLED TO DEPOSE DEFENDANT EVERS, AS HE IS A CENTRAL PARTY TO THE CASE. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANT WOULD LIKE TO DEFER HIS DEPOSITION UNTIL 10 DAYS AFTER HE IS REQUIRED TO FILE AN ANSWER TO THE COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF'S PREFERENCE TO DEPOSE DEFENDANT SOONER IS PERMITTED BY THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE. THE FACT THAT ISSUES RELATING TO THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE AND/OR THE LENGTH OF THE DEPOSITION MAY ARISE DURING THE DEPOSITION DOES NOT MEAN THAT THERE IS NO VALID PURPOSE FOR THE DEPOSITION OR OTHERWISE WARRANT THE REQUESTED PROTECTIVE ORDER AT THIS TIME. TO THE EXTENT THAT THE MOTION SEEKS PROSPECTIVE RELIEF FOR ANTICIPATED ISSUES, IT IS PREMATURE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM KATHERINE HUIBONHOA, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

HEARING - R U L I N G: Plaintiff JOSEPH SCHATZ'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Elliot B. Evers To 1) Appear For Deposition And Produce Documents 2) Answer Form Interrogatories, Set 1; And 3) Answer Request For Admissions, Set One is Granted. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Huibonhoa; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-23-2013 Continued To Nov-20-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Oct-02-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHRISTOPHER C. NOLAN OF THE BRENT, FIOL & NOLAN LLP, TEL. NO. 984-0416 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ELLIOT EVERS'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT is granted. The Court will consider the late filed opposition filed by plaintiff. Defense counsel demonstrates excusable neglect. The Court denies plaintiff's request for sanctions. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Mtn For Protective Order
Off Calendar Sep-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances, Plaintiff Joseph L. Schatz in Pro Per present (415-677-9151) opposing party. Atty. Christofer C Nolan of Brent of Fiol & Nolan LLP (415-984-0416) moving party.

Defendant's counsel Christofer Nolan did not give proper notice of an intention to appear to contest the tentative ruling. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ELLIOT EVERS' Motion For Protective Order is Denied. Motion is largely moot because the Court has overruled Defendant Evers' demurrer (see August 29, 2013 Order Overruling Defendant Evers' Demurrer To Complaint, notice of entry into the docket on September 4, 2013). Plaintiff Schatz is entitled to depose Defendant Evers, as he is a central party to the case. Although Defendant would like to defer his deposition until 10 days after he is required to file an Answer to the Complaint, Plaintiff's preference to depose defendant sooner is permitted by the Code of Civil Procedure. The fact that issues relating to the attorney-client privilege and/or the length of the deposition may arise during the deposition does not mean that there is no valid purpose for the deposition or otherwise warrant the requested protective order at this time. To the extent that the motion seeks prospective relief for anticipated issues, it is premature. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Katherine Huibonhoa, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

Mtn For Protective Order
Off Calendar Sep-04-2013 Continued To Sep-11-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances on this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Defendant ELLIOT EVERS' Motion for Protective Order is Continued to September 11, 2013 to give Mr. Evers the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Mr. Evers must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than September 4, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Judge Pro Tem: Charles O. Geerhart; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Christopher C. Nolan, Esq. of Brent Fiol & Nolan, LLP, Tel. 415.984.0416, for defendant and moving party;
Joseph L. Schatz, Esq. of the Law Office of Joseph Schatz, Tel. 415.677.9151, plaintiff and opposing party appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ELLIOT EVERS'S DEMURRER TO the entire COMPLAINT is overruled. The complaint fails to show that the 2-year statute of limitations bars this action. The allegations in the complaint regarding fraud and mistake indicate that the 3-year statute of limitations should apply under CCP 338(d). Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-12-2013 Continued To Oct-09-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
